title: Listening - Part 4: Life in Dance Companies
description: You will hear five short extracts in which professional dancers talk about their life in dance companies. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I've been with the company for six years now, and you'd think the nerves would vanish, but [21]knowing that there’ll be reviews of my performance – that’s still enough to set me off[/21]. I find myself obsessing over what the critics might say about my technique in the morning papers. On the plus side, it's been a rewarding path. [26]I do get a kick out of being recognised in the street[/26] or having fans wait for an autograph after a show; it makes all the hard work feel worth it.||Speaker 2: Every time I'm in the wings, I start doing these extra stretches, almost compulsively. [22]I wonder what I’d do if I strained something so badly that it’d put me out of action[/22]. The fear of a sudden snap or a twist is always there. However, being part of a touring company has its perks. [27]I never thought I’d see so many unusual places[/27] as I have in the last two years. From remote towns in Asia to major European capitals, the travel has been an education in itself.||Speaker 3: There was this one time early in my career where [23]my mind went blank[/23] right in the middle of a solo, and [23]that still haunts me when I’m waiting in the wings[/23]. I'm terrified that one day the steps just won't be there when the music starts. But I love the artistic side of the job. [28]Dancing to the works of such a wide range of composers enriches my life[/28] deeply. Whether it's modern minimalist beats or classical symphonies, the musical variety keeps me inspired day after day.||Speaker 4: I'm naturally quite a solitary person, so [24]I get flustered thinking that I’m part of a group and they rely on me[/24] to be in the right place at the right time. The idea of causing a collision or ruining a formation for everyone else is what gets me. But what I really value is the creative freedom we're given. Working with [29]young choreographers who use experimental designs[/29] and [29]pushing boundaries[/29] in modern dance is what keeps the job fresh for me.||Speaker 5: I have a bit of a ritual before I go on. [25]I'm neurotic about being stuck in front of an audience without something crucial to my role[/25], so I'll check my shoes, my ribbons, and my props three or four times. I just dread that something will be missing once the curtain goes up. Outside of performing, I've started running workshops for kids in the local community. [30]I get such a buzz from being a role model[/30] and seeing their faces light up when they finally grasp a movement. It's great to pass on that passion.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) how each speaker feels before they go on stage.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 is apprehensive about reviews and critics' opinions.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 worries about physical strains or injuries that could end their career.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 is haunted by a past event where they forgot their steps.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 is anxious about their responsibility to the rest of the group.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 triple-checks equipment and props, fearing they might be missing something.

options.A: excited by the prospect of a live audience
options.C: worried about forgetting what to do
options.E: frustrated by the lack of adequate rehearsal time
options.F: fearful of not having the right equipment
options.G: overwhelmed by the scale of the venue

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker appreciates about their career.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 enjoys being recognised by people in the street.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 values seeing 'unusual places' while on tour.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 appreciates the variety of musical composers they work with.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 likes working with innovative, experimental choreographers.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 enjoys being a role model for young dancers in workshops.

options.D: the close friendships formed with fellow dancers
options.F: the exposure to a variety of music
options.G: the satisfaction of mastering difficult techniques
options.H: the chance to inspire young dancers
